title: The Everrain - a detailed unboxing of the base Kickstarter pledge
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2vAcCPS9-YE

Daniel (@dungeondive) finally unboxes The Everrain after roughly four years and a Kickstarter saga of delays and a mid-campaign shipping-fund shortfall — drama that hasn't dented his excitement for its drowned-world, nautical Cthulhu weird-fiction theme. He runs the base pledge plus three small-box expansions (The Seafarer, Twilight Flood, Undertow of Madness), reading the fiction of an endless rain swallowing the world. It's a co-op, story-driven exploration game for one to four: captain your own customisable ship, hire crew with randomly generated traits, plunder shipwrecks, and fight ship-to-ship and on-deck across a procedurally generated island map. True to form, he grumbles about wasted box space, a neoprene mat printed at the wrong dimensions so its tiles don't align, and an unnecessarily seam-lined boss mini he'd rather have as a token. He's happier with the card count than he feared — 91 sea events, expeditions, denizens — and flags his plan to raid these gorgeous weird-fantasy minis for Pirate Borg, Skull Tales and Mork Borg games. Worker-placement-ish crew, navigation and artillery orders round out a system he's heard is a rough teach but is eager to actually play.

----

The Everrain is a game Daniel waited four years for, through Kickstarter delays and a shipping-fund shortfall. Does a long, messy crowdfunding wait sour a game before it hits the table, or does anticipation make the payoff sweeter?
